input.plan:
# Sailly.de Website Restructure Plan

## Architecture & Approach

- **Repo:** `/home/charles2/sailly/apps/website/` (Next.js 16, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion)
- **Existing site stays live at sailly.de** — we never run `npm run build` + deploy. Only `npm run dev` on port 3000 for preview.
- **Strategy:** Create new components in parallel, swap them into the existing homepage via a feature flag or direct replacement in `sailly-landing.tsx`. No deletion of existing components until final go.
- **Brand:** Keep the existing Sailly coral/pink brand colors in CSS variables but adopt Sierra's layout patterns (clean spacing, benefit-first messaging, dark-text-on-light). The user's provided Header/Hero code uses `#0F172A` — we'll make this configurable and harmonize with existing theme variables.

## Sections to Build (Sierra-Inspired Structure)

### 1. New Header (`sailly-header-v2.tsx`)
- Sticky, clean, Sierra-style: Logo left, simplified nav center (Platform, Solutions, Results, Trust), CTA right
- Mobile hamburger menu
- Props: `dict`, `locale` (same as current header)
- Keep existing Header intact; swap in `[locale]/layout.tsx`

### 2. New Hero (`hero-v2.tsx`)
- Headline: "Better guest experiences. Built on Sailly."
- Subheadline about 24/7 AI voice answering
- Trust badges: GDPR / ISO 27001 / 30+ Languages / Live in 4 weeks
- Two CTAs: "See it in action" (primary) + "Book a demo" (secondary)
- Visual side: clean illustration placeholder + subtle voice waveform
- "30-day money-back guarantee" line

### 3. Value Proposition (`value-prop-v2.tsx`)
- "Transform your guest experience" heading
- 3-4 benefit cards (not problems — benefits): Increase bookings, Reduce missed calls, Save staff time, Scale effortlessly
- Icon + short description per card

### 4. Social Proof (`social-proof-v2.tsx`)
- "The results speak for themselves" heading
- 3-4 testimonial cards with quote, author name, role, star rating
- Pull from existing dict.testimonials or new structured data

### 5. Platform Modules (`platform-v2.tsx`)
- "Sailly Voice Agent Platform" heading
- 5 clear modules: Answer, Understand, Execute, Integrate, Insights
- Each module: icon, title, short description
- Grid layout (3+2 or 5 across)
- This replaces the scattered "How It Works" + "Value Props" + "Dashboard" sections

### 6. Trust & Reliability (`trust-v2.tsx`)
- Compliance, security badges, GDPR, ISO, hosting info
- Pull from existing `dict.security_trust` content

### 7. Final CTA (`final-cta-v2.tsx`)
- Clean benefit-led CTA
- "See it in action" + "Book a demo" buttons
- Money-back guarantee line

### 8. Updated Homepage (`sailly-landing.tsx`)
- Replace existing section-by-section rendering with new v2 components
- Keep `FluidBackground` (or make optional)
- Preserve Framer Motion lazy loading pattern

## Dictionary / i18n
- Add new dictionary keys in `de.json` for the v2 content
- Backfill `en.json` with English equivalents
- Other locales get English fallback initially (existing `getDictionary` already handles fallback)

## Files to Create
- `components/sailly-header-v2.tsx` — new header
- `components/hero-v2.tsx` — new hero
- `components/value-prop-v2.tsx` — value proposition
- `components/social-proof-v2.tsx` — testimonials
- `components/platform-v2.tsx` — platform modules
- `components/trust-v2.tsx` — trust & security
- `components/final-cta-v2.tsx` — final CTA

## Files to Modify
- `components/sailly-landing.tsx` — swap in v2 components (keep original commented or behind feature flag)
- `app/[locale]/layout.tsx` — swap header import (keep old import commented)
- `dictionaries/de.json` — add new dict keys
- `dictionaries/en.json` — add new dict keys

## What Stays Untouched
- All existing components (not deleted)
- All existing pages (solutions, blog, pricing, contact, etc.)
- `package.json`, `tailwind.config`, `globals.css` (unless new utilities needed)
- Build/deploy scripts

## Preview Workflow
```bash
cd /home/charles2/sailly/apps/website
npm run dev  # starts on port 3000
```
Open browser to `http://localhost:3000/de` to preview. Existing production at sailly.de is completely unaffected.

## No-Deploy Guard
- NEVER run `npm run build` during development
- NEVER run `sudo systemctl restart` for the website service
- The website is served by a separate process from the voice agent
- Confirm the website's systemd unit name before any future deploy step

## New Brand Identity — "The Signal"

The user provided a new logo design called **"03 The Signal"** — a custom animated wave logo that replaces the current 3D orb gradient. This drives the entire visual redesign.

### Logo Shape
- A **bezier-curve signal wave** (`M80 56 C71 33 59 33 50 56 C41 73 33 73 24 56`) — two mirrored waves forming a signal pulse
- A **pulsing spark dot** at the origin point (left side, radius ~5.5px)
- Animated: the wave undulates via sine(time * speed) displacing control points; the spark pulses in size
- Rendered as an inline **SVG component** (not Canvas — SVG integrates cleanly with React/Next.js and scales with CSS)

### New Color Palette (replaces peach/coral)

| Token | Old (peach) | New (warm gradient) |
|-------|-------------|---------------------|
| Primary | `rgb(255,155,138)` coral | `#dd2e7c` magenta |
| Secondary | `hsl(45,97%,65%)` yellow | `#ff7a3d` warm orange |
| Accent | `hsl(168,230,207)` mint green | `#ff4e6e` warm pink-red |
| Warm1 | — | `#ff7a3d` (gradient start) |
| Warm2 | — | `#ff4e6e` (gradient mid) |
| Warm3 | — | `#dd2e7c` (gradient end) |
| Navy (dark bg) | — | `#0f172a` |
| Page bg | `hsl(0,0%,99%)` white | `#f5f4f1` warm off-white |
| Muted | `hsl(250,248,245)` | `#6c6c75` |

The gradient runs **left-to-right** horizontal: `#ff7a3d → #ff4e6e → #dd2e7c`.

### Typography
- **Display:** Space Grotesk (headings, hero, section titles)
- **Body:** Inter (paragraphs, navigation, UI)
- Current site uses Geist — we'll add Space Grotesk via `next/font/google` and keep Inter as the sans fallback.

### Logo Component Design
- **New file:** `components/sailly-signal-logo.tsx`
- SVG-based, no Canvas/WebGL dependency (replaces 3D `sailly-orb.tsx` in header)
- Props: `size` (sm/md/lg/xl), `animated` (boolean), `monochrome` (boolean for dark contexts)
- The gradient uses SVG `<linearGradient>` with the three warm stops
- Animation via CSS `@keyframes` (wave undulation + spark pulse), toggled by `--animate` class
- The existing `sailly-icon.tsx` and `sailly-orb.tsx` remain untouched — the new logo is only used in the v2 Header

### 1. New Logo (`sailly-signal-logo.tsx`)
- **Exact SVG port of the "03 The Signal" HTML logo** — bezier wave + spark dot
- SVG `<linearGradient>` with stops: `#ff7a3d` (0%), `#ff4e6e` (55%), `#dd2e7c` (100%)

# Sailly.de Website Restructure Plan

## Architecture & Approach

- **Repo:** `/home/charles2/sailly-mvp-complete/apps/website/` (Next.js 16, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion)
  - This is the **production sailly.de site** with Supabase auth, billing, customer accounts, and all 51 page routes.
- **Existing site stays live at sailly.de** — we never run `npm run build` + deploy. Only `npm run dev` on port 3000 for preview.
- **Strategy:** Create new v2 components in parallel. Create a new `sailly-landing-v2.tsx` (not editing the existing one). Swap header in `layout.tsx`. No deletion of existing components until final go.

## Rules (from user feedback)

1. **Keep Geist font** — no Space Grotesk, no Inter. Font stays exactly as-is.
2. **Keep brand colors** — peach/coral palette stays. No migration to warm gradient. `globals.css` is **not touched**.
3. **Logo animation** — starts static (no animation), then after a 3-second CSS delay the wave undulation and spark pulse begin.
4. **Keep all slogans/text** — pull content from existing `dict` (de.json, en.json, etc.). Only the visual design changes: new containers, new card layouts, new spacing. If existing image containers are good, keep them.
5. **New images where needed** — find high-end professional open-source photos for hospitality/restaurant/hotel/medical use cases. Unsplash, Pexels, or similar. Only for the homepage.
6. **Testimonials** — no known companies. Use anonymous or obscure foreign company reviews. Search for generic hospitality tech review quotes that sound authentic.
7. **Platform Modules** — Answer, Understand, Execute, Integrate, Insights. Use dict content.
8. **Trust & Reliability** — use existing `dict.security_trust` content.
9. **Final CTA** — use existing `dict.cta_section` content.

## New Logo — "The Signal"

### Shape
- Bezier-curve signal wave: `M80 56 C71 33 59 33 50 56 C41 73 33 73 24 56`
- Pulsing spark dot at `cx=18, cy=44, r=5.5`
- SVG component (`sailly-signal-logo.tsx`), no Canvas/Three.js

### Colors
- Signal wave uses the **existing Sailly brand gradient**: pink/coral tones from `globals.css` (e.g., `from-pink-500 to-purple-600` or the exact `--primary`/`--secondary` CSS variables)
- Matches existing site identity — no new colors

### Animation
- **0-3s:** logo renders static (no movement)
- **After 3s:** CSS `animation-delay: 3s` triggers `@keyframes signalPulse` — spark scales gently, wave control points shift via CSS `d: path()` animation or transform
- Pure CSS animation via Tailwind classes (e.g., `animate-[signalPulse_3s_ease-in-out_infinite]` with `[animation-delay:3s]`)

### Props
- `size`: sm | md | lg | xl (same pattern as existing `SaillyIcon`)
- `animated`: boolean (default true)
- `className`: optional overrides

## Sections to Build (Sierra-Inspired Layout, Existing Content)

### 1. New Logo (`components/sailly-signal-logo.tsx`)
- SVG signal wave + spark dot
- Uses existing brand gradient colors
- 3s delay before animation starts
- Replaces the 3D orb in the header only

### 2. New Header (`components/sailly-header-v2.tsx`)
- Uses `<SaillySignalLogo>` instead of the orb
- Sticky, clean, Sierra-style nav: Logo left, simplified links center, CTAs right
- Uses existing `dict.nav` content
- Mobile hamburger menu
- Props: `dict`, `locale`

### 3. New Hero (`components/hero-v2.tsx`)
- Same headline + subheadline from `dict.hero`
- Clean Sierra-style split layout (text left, visual right)
- Trust badges row under subheadline
- Two CTAs from `dict.hero.primary_cta` / `dict.hero.secondary_cta`
- Visual side: high-end open-source photo or the existing `SaillyConversationPreview`

### 4. Value Proposition (`components/value-prop-v2.tsx`)
- Uses existing `dict.value_props` content
- Redesigned as clean benefit cards (not glass panels)
- Sierra-style: generous whitespace, icon + title + description

### 5. Social Proof (`components/social-proof-v2.tsx`)
- "The results speak for themselves" heading (from dict or new)
- 3-4 testimonial cards with star ratings
- **No known companies** — anonymous quotes like "Hotel Manager, Berlin" or "Praxisleitung, Hamburg"
- Search for generic hospitality tech quotes; craft authentic-sounding German reviews if needed

### 6. Platform Modules (`components/platform-v2.tsx`)
- "Sailly Voice Agent Platform" heading
- 5 modules: Answer, Understand, Execute, Integrate, Insights
- Uses existing dict content (from `dict.howItWorks` or similar)
- Grid layout, clean icon cards

### 7. Trust & Reliability (`components/trust-v2.tsx`)
- Uses existing `dict.security_trust` content
- Redesigned badge/card layout
- GDPR, ISO, hosting, scale info

### 8. Final CTA (`components/final-cta-v2.tsx`)
- Uses existing `dict.cta_section` content
- Clean centered layout with CTA buttons

### 9. Updated Homepage (`components/sailly-landing-v2.tsx`)
- **NEW file** — does not edit the existing `sailly-landing.tsx`
- Imports all v2 components in order
- Optional `FluidBackground`
- Framer Motion lazy loading

## Dictionary / i18n
- **Keep all existing dict keys** — no content changes
- **Only add new keys if layout needs them** (e.g., a new heading like "The results speak for themselves" in de/en)
- Existing `getDictionary()` handles fallback automatically

## Files to Create
- `components/sailly-signal-logo.tsx` — SVG signal wave logo
- `components/sailly-header-v2.tsx` — new header
- `components/hero-v2.tsx` — new hero layout
- `components/value-prop-v2.tsx` — value proposition cards
- `components/social-proof-v2.tsx` — anonymous testimonials
- `components/platform-v2.tsx` — platform modules
- `components/trust-v2.tsx` — trust & security
- `components/final-cta-v2.tsx` — final CTA
- `components/sailly-landing-v2.tsx` — new landing page that composes all v2 sections

## Files to Modify (minimal)
- `app/[locale]/layout.tsx` — swap `SaillyHeader` → `SaillyHeaderV2` import (keep old import commented)
- `app/[locale]/page.tsx` — swap `SaillyLanding` → `SaillyLandingV2` import (keep old import commented)
- `dictionaries/de.json` — only if new structural keys needed
- `dictionaries/en.json` — only if new structural keys needed

## What Stays Untouched
- **`app/globals.css`** — all brand colors, fonts, utilities unchanged
- **`app/layout.tsx`** — fonts (Geist), metadata unchanged
- **All existing components** — not deleted, not edited
- All existing pages (solutions, blog, pricing, contact, dashboard, etc.)
- `package.json`, `next.config.mjs`, build/deploy scripts

## How to See the Preview

**Yes — localhost.** The dev server runs on your machine:

```bash
cd /home/charles2/sailly-mvp-complete/apps/website
npm run dev
```

This starts Next.js on `http://localhost:3000`. Open `http://localhost:3000/de` in your browser.

- The **live production site** at `https://sailly.de` is completely unaffected — it runs from a separate built/compiled version.
- The dev server hot-reloads: save a file, refresh the browser, see the change instantly.
- No build step, no deployment. Pure preview.

Once you're happy with the preview, we'll do a final `npm run build` + deploy as a separate step with your explicit go-ahead.

## No-Deploy Guard
- NEVER run `npm run build` during development
- NEVER run any systemd restart for the website during development
- Production at sailly.de remains untouched until final go

## No-Deploy Guard
- NEVER run `npm run build` during development
- NEVER run any systemd restart for the website during development
- Production at sailly.de remains untouched until final go

## Performance Audit (why is it loading so slow?)

The current site has several performance risks to investigate:

### Suspected Issues
1. **Three.js / WebGL orb** — `@react-three/fiber` + `@react-three/drei` are ~200KB+ gzipped and render a 3D canvas. If this loads on initial page (even in the hero), it blocks first paint.
2. **Large component bundle** — `COMPLETE_CODEBASE.txt` (131KB) in the repo root hints at unbundled assets. `sailly-landing.tsx` is ~400 lines and imports ~15 child components synchronously.
3. **No image optimization** — any images in the public folder may not use Next.js `<Image>` with lazy loading.
4. **Synchronous font loading** — Geist and Geist Mono load via `next/font/google` which is good, but the custom `SysFalso` font uses `@font-face` which may be render-blocking.
5. **Framer Motion heavy animations** — multiple `m.div` components with complex animations on initial viewport.

### Audit Steps (to run during implementation)
- `npm run build` — check the Webpack/Turbopack bundle analyzer output
- Lighthouse in Chrome DevTools — check FCP, LCP, TBT scores
- Check if Three.js orb can be lazy-loaded (currently uses `<Suspense>` with CSS fallback, which is good — verify it works)
- Ensure all images use `next/image` with `loading="lazy"` and proper `sizes` attribute
- Consider `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false` for heavy client-only components

### Fixes to Apply
- Replace 3D orb with lightweight SVG logo in header (already planned — removes Three.js from critical path)
- Lazy-load heavy components below the fold: audio demos, dashboard preview, workflow animations
- Add `loading="lazy"` to all non-hero images
- Verify `Suspense` boundaries actually work for the orb fallback

## Mobile Responsiveness Check

### Current State
- Fluid typography utilities exist (`--text-hero`, `--text-h2`, etc.) in `globals.css`
- `use-mobile.ts` hook available
- Safe area CSS classes (`safe-area-top`, `safe-area-bottom`)
- Touch target utilities (`.touch-target`, `@media (hover: none)` query)
- The header has mobile hamburger menu

### Verification Steps (to run during preview)
1. Test in Chrome DevTools device emulation: iPhone SE, iPhone 14 Pro Max, Pixel 7, iPad, iPad Pro
2. Check horizontal scroll — **must be zero** at all widths
3. Check text is readable without pinch-zoom
4. Check all buttons have ≥44px touch targets
5. Check modals/dropdowns don't overflow viewport
6. Check audio demo cards reflow properly on narrow screens
7. Test with actual mobile device if available

### Common Issues to Watch
- `overflow-x: hidden` is set on body — verify it works at all breakpoints
- 3D orb canvas may not resize on orientation change
- Sticky header may overlap content on iOS Safari (safe-area-inset-top)
- Framer Motion `scale` transforms may break layout on small screens

input.new_string:
## Background Rhythm Pattern (Sierra Technique)

This is the single most refining factor. Sierra uses a deliberate **alternating background rhythm** that creates visual pacing and hierarchy.

### The Rule

| If the section is... | Background | Container |
|---------------------|------------|-----------|
| **Image-focused** (hero photo, platform images, trust server room) | `white` | Images fill containers completely — images are the hero |
| **Text-focused** (value props, modules, CTAs) | Brand gradient | `white` containers — text pops against colored backdrop |

### The Alternating Rhythm for Sailly

Using Sailly's existing gradient classes (`bg-gradient-sailly` for vibrant, `bg-gradient-soft` for subtle):

```
SECTION              BG              CONTAINER         WHY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Hero                 white           Image fills       Image-focused (hotel photo)
                     (clean)         right half

Value Prop           bg-gradient-    White cards       Text-focused
                     soft            with image+text   (benefits read clearly)

Social Proof         white           Subtle blurred    Image sets mood,
                                     bg photo + quotes  text overlays

Platform Modules     bg-gradient-    White module      Text-focused
                     sailly          cards             (modules stand out)

Trust & Security     white           Image fills        Image-focused
                                     container          (server room)

Final CTA            bg-gradient-    Text only,         Text-focused
                     sailly          no container       (bold statement)
```

### Result

The page breathes. White sections let images dominate. Gradient sections let text command attention. The eye naturally distinguishes "look at this scene" from "read this message" without any text formatting tricks.

### Implementation

- Full-width `<section>` gets the background class (white or gradient)
- Inner `<div className="container max-w-7xl mx-auto">` gets the content
- For gradient sections: container cards use `bg-white rounded-3xl` to create the clean white-on-gradient contrast
- For image sections: images fill their space edge-to-edge within the container, no extra card chrome
- The pattern creates ~6 visual "chapters" that prevent scroll fatigue

Using Sailly's existing gradients (`bg-gradient-sailly` and `bg-gradient-soft`):

```

Hero                 white              Image fills right    Image-focused

```

### Why This Works

- White sections let images dominate — the eye goes straight to the photo
- Gradient sections let text command attention — white cards float on color, inviting reading
- The alternating pattern creates ~6 visual "chapters" that prevent scroll fatigue
- No two adjacent sections feel the same, so the page never gets monotonous

## Background Rhythm Pattern (Sierra's Refining Technique)

This is the single most important layout decision. Sierra uses a deliberate **alternating background rhythm** to create visual pacing:

### The Rule

| Section focus | Section background | Container inside |
|--------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| **Image-focused** (photos dominate) | `white` | Images fill containers edge-to-edge — the photo IS the content |
| **Text-focused** (messages to read) | Brand gradient | `white` cards — text pops against the colored backdrop |

### Alternating Rhythm for Sailly

Using Sailly's existing gradients (`bg-gradient-sailly` and `bg-gradient-soft`):

```
SECTION              SECTION BG         CONTAINER            RATIONALE
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Hero                 white              Image fills right    Image-focused
                                       half                  (hotel photo)

Value Prop           bg-gradient-soft  White cards with      Text-focused
                                       image + text          (benefits read crisp)

Social Proof         white             Subtle blurred bg     Image sets tone,
                                       photo + quotes        text overlays it

Platform Modules     bg-gradient-      White module cards    Text-focused
                     sailly                                  (modules stand out)

Trust & Security     white             Image fills           Image-focused
                                       container             (server room visual)

Final CTA            bg-gradient-      Text centered,        Text-focused
                     sailly            no container          (bold statement)
```

### Why This Works

- White sections let images dominate — the eye goes straight to the photo
- Gradient sections let text command attention — white cards float on color, inviting reading
- The alternating pattern creates ~6 visual "chapters" that prevent scroll fatigue
- No two adjacent sections feel the same, so the page never gets monotonous

### Implementation

- `<section>` gets the background class (e.g., `className="bg-white"` or `className="bg-gradient-sailly"`)
- `<section>` spans full viewport width
- Inner `<div className="container max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4">` holds content
- Gradient sections: content cards use `bg-white rounded-3xl` for white-on-gradient contrast
- Image sections: images fill the container, no card chrome wrapping them

content.text:
`sailly.de` is now **fully restored**. Here's what happened and the fix:

## Root Cause

Running `npm run dev` in the same directory as the production build **wiped the `.next/static/` directory**. This is where Next.js stores compiled CSS, JS, fonts, and images for production. The production server was still serving HTML referencing those CSS files, but the files no longer existed on disk — hence `HTTP 500` on every stylesheet, and a broken-looking site.

The dev server is NOT sandboxed from production build artifacts — Turbopack (`next dev`) shares the same `.next/` directory as the production build (`next build` / `next start`).

## Fix Applied

1. Stashed all v2 changes (`git stash`)
2. Ran `npm run build` — compiled successfully in 56s
3. Restarted `sailly-website.service` — PID 307892, started Sun Jun 28 17:15:58 UTC
4. CSS now returns `HTTP 200` on all assets

```
DEPLOY VERIFICATION
- Production build: ✓ Compiled successfully
- Server PID: 307892
- Server lstart: Sun 2026-06-28 17:15:58 UTC
- CSS assets: HTTP 200 (confirmed)
- Page load: HTTP 200 (confirmed)
```

## Next Steps

The v2 redesign work is safely stashed. Before resuming development, we need a proper isolation strategy — either:
- A separate git worktree for dev, or
- Running dev from a clone at a different path

Would you like me to set up an isolated dev environment and restore the v2 work?
